_protocol: Phase 3.5-PRAGMATIC: 5+red+5 fetches with adversarial gap review, then operator LR commit with per-LR citations

extracted.tariff_view: one-time price-level increase, fades over ~2 quarters
extracted.labor_view: expanding solidly but job gains subdued; U-3 stable
extracted.reaction_function_acknowledgment: Powell explicitly acknowledged market's interpretation of Fed reaction function is broadly accurate

extracted.passthrough_timing: cumulative effects 7 months post-implementation; full dollar-for-dollar pass-through
extracted.historical_lesson_40yr_intl_data: tariffs initially raise unemployment + lower inflation, then unemployment normalizes while inflation increases

extracted.political_context: Trump-nominated members (Bowman, Waller, Miran) lean dovish; Trump attempting to fire some Biden nominees (Cook); slight hawkish tilt overall but with significant internal divisions

gaps_identified: Current labor market state (U-3, NFP, JOLTS) needed to anchor H3 base rate
Current core PCE level + 3m/6m trajectory needed to anchor H1/H2 split
Powell succession / May 2026 Chair transition is structural regime-change risk for all hypotheses
Treasury issuance + 10y term premium + financial conditions index — non-Fed channel that forces Fed's hand
SEP dot historical accuracy — Goodhart check on Source 1's predictive weight

extracted.trend_vs_2pct_target: PERSISTENTLY ELEVATED — 3.2% above 3.0% threshold of t2_core_pce_above_3 indicator (effectively already firing)
extracted._key_implication: Currently in core_pce_above_3 zone, NOT in sticky 2.5-3.0 band; H2 cut path requires PCE cooling first

extracted.warsh_historical_orientation: Fed governor 2006-2011, historically hawkish, dissented from QE
extracted.current_political_alignment: Trump-nominated, expected to be more responsive to political pressure for cuts than Powell
extracted._key_implication: Mid-year Chair regime change. From May 15 onward, FOMC reaction function shifts. H4 (hike) becomes much less likely under Trump-aligned Chair; H3 (>=2 cuts) becomes more likely if Trump pressure dominates
extracted.powell_remains_on_board: yes — for unspecified period; continuing voting member

extracted.fed_h15_release_date: 2026-05-01 (most recent Federal Reserve H.15 selected interest rates)
extracted.acm_term_premium_data_freshness: FRED THREEFYTP10 series; ACM model from NY Fed; most recent visible value April 2025
extracted._key_implication: Specific term premium / NFCI level not extracted in this fetch — requires direct FRED query in next session for clean numerical anchor

extracted.powell_self_assessment: 'The dots are not a great forecaster of future rate moves'
limitations_cited: Fed influences but doesn't control economy
tariffs/conflicts can swift change short-term policy
extracted._key_implication: SEP dot evidence should be weighted MODERATELY, not heavily. Indicators t3_sep_dot_plot_revised_lower and t3_sep_dots_unchanged should have modest LRs.

topic_priors.rationale: Source 3 (Warsh succession) shifts H4 down (hike unlikely under Trump-aligned chair) and H2/H3 up (Trump pressure for cuts). Source 2 (current core PCE 3.2%) still constrains aggressive cutting in near term — moderating the H2/H3 increase. Source 1 (SEP median = 1 cut, range widened) supports H2 as modal.

key_revisions_from_priors.powell_tone_indicators: DESCRIPTIONS UPDATED: now apply to current Chair (Powell through 2026-05-15, Warsh after). Both interpret on-record cut/hold/hawkish language. Warsh-era language likely more political-economy-aware than Powell-era technocratic neutrality.

_lr_commit_summary.per_indicator_citations: see indicator desc fields in build_phase3.py
